title: X-Men

**Current:** [Beast](/wiki/Beast_(Marvel_Comics) "Beast (Marvel Comics)") [Cyclops](/wiki/Cyclops_(Marvel_Comics) "Cyclops (Marvel Comics)") [Gambit](/wiki/Gambit_(Marvel_Comics) "Gambit (Marvel Comics)") [Glob Herman](/wiki/Glob_Herman "Glob Herman") [Jen Starkey](/wiki/Animalia_(character) "Animalia (character)") [Jubilee](/wiki/Jubilee_(character) "Jubilee (character)") [Juggernaut](/wiki/Juggernaut_(character) "Juggernaut (character)") [Kid Omega](/wiki/Kid_Omega "Kid Omega") [Magik](/wiki/Magik "Magik") [Magneto](/wiki/Magneto_(Marvel_Comics) "Magneto (Marvel Comics)") [Nightcrawler](/wiki/Nightcrawler_(character) "Nightcrawler (character)") [Psylocke](/wiki/Kwannon_(character) "Kwannon (character)") [Rogue](/wiki/Rogue_(Marvel_Comics) "Rogue (Marvel Comics)") [Temper](/wiki/Oya_(comics) "Oya (comics)") [Wolverine](/wiki/Wolverine_(character) "Wolverine (character)") [Xorn](/wiki/Xorn "Xorn") **Former:** See *[List of X-Men members](/wiki/List_of_X-Men_members "List of X-Men members")*

• The Factory Merle, Alaska • Haven House New Orleans, Louisiana **Former:** • [Xavier's School for Gifted Youngsters](/wiki/X-Mansion "X-Mansion")/Jean Grey School for Higher Learning/Xavier Institute for Mutant Education and Outreach [Westchester County, New York](/wiki/Westchester_County,_New_York "Westchester County, New York") • Cooterman's Creek Australian Outback • Utopia San Francisco, California • The Treehouse ([Krakoan](/wiki/Krakoa "Krakoa") base) [New York City](/wiki/New_York_City "New York City") • The [Morlocks](/wiki/Morlocks_(comics) "Morlocks (comics)")' Alley New York Sewer System [New York City](/wiki/New_York_City "New York City") |

are a [superhero](/wiki/Superhero "Superhero") team in [American comic books](/wiki/American_comic_book "American comic book") published by [Marvel Comics](/wiki/Marvel_Comics "Marvel Comics"). Created by writer/editor [Stan Lee](/wiki/Stan_Lee "Stan Lee") and artist/co-plotter [Jack Kirby](/wiki/Jack_Kirby "Jack Kirby"), the team first appeared in [*The X-Men*](/wiki/Uncanny_X-Men "Uncanny X-Men") #1 (September 1963).[[1]](#cite_note-1) Although initially cancelled in 1970 due to low sales, following its [1975 revival](/wiki/Giant-Size_X-Men "Giant-Size X-Men") and subsequent direction under writer [Chris Claremont](/wiki/Chris_Claremont "Chris Claremont"), it became one of Marvel Comics's most recognizable and successful franchises.[[2]](#cite_note-2) They have appeared in numerous books, [television shows](/wiki/X-Men_in_television "X-Men in television"), [20th Century Fox](/wiki/20th_Century_Studios "20th Century Studios")'s [*X-Men* films](/wiki/X-Men_(film_series) "X-Men (film series)"), and [video games](/wiki/List_of_video_games_featuring_the_X-Men "List of video games featuring the X-Men"). The *X-Men* title may refer to the superhero team itself, [the eponymous comic series](/wiki/X-Men_(comic_book) "X-Men (comic book)"), or the broader franchise, which includes [various solo titles and team books](/wiki/List_of_X-Men_comics "List of X-Men comics"), such as the [New Mutants](/wiki/New_Mutants "New Mutants"), [Excalibur](/wiki/Excalibur_(comics) "Excalibur (comics)"), and [X-Force](/wiki/X-Force "X-Force").

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Marvel Comics superhero team of mutants

This article is about the superhero team. For other uses, see [X-Men (disambiguation)](/wiki/X-Men_(disambiguation) "X-Men (disambiguation)").

Not to be confused with [X-Man](/wiki/X-Man "X-Man").
